    correct_answer: 'A',
    distractor_analysis: {
      B: 'forgets_to_add_2_when_distributing_the_subtraction',

    difficulty: 1,

    sub_skill_id: 'M-09',
    difficulty: 2,
    question_text: 'A population of 500 rabbits triples every 2 years. Which expression represents the population after t years?',
    passage_id: null,

    correct_answer: 'A',
    distractor_analysis: {
      B: 'inverts_the_exponent_causing_decay_to_be_far_too_rapid',
      C: 'uses_growth_base_2_instead_of_decay_base_0.5',
      D: 'models_linear_decay_instead_of_exponential_decay'
    },
    is_ai_generated: false,
    tags: []
  },
  // M-10 diff 3
  {
    question_id: 'q_m10_004',
    source: 'seed_data',
    section: 'math',
    sub_skill_id: 'M-10',
    difficulty: 3,
    question_text: 'A recipe calls for 2 cups of flour for every 3 cups of sugar. How many cups of sugar are needed if a baker uses 8 cups of flour?',
    passage_id: null,
    answer_choices: { A: '12', B: '10', C: '6', D: '16' },
    correct_answer: 'A',
    distractor_analysis: {
      B: 'adds_2_to_8_then_adds_2_more_rather_than_scaling_the_ratio',
      C: 'inverts_the_ratio_dividing_8_by_the_flour_amount',
      D: 'multiplies_8_by_2_instead_of_scaling_by_3_over_2'
    },
    is_ai_generated: false,
    tags: []
  },
  // M-10 diff 5
  {
    question_id: 'q_m10_005',
    source: 'seed_data',
    section: 'math',
    sub_skill_id: 'M-10',
    difficulty: 5,
    question_text: 'A train travels from City A to City B at 60 mph and returns at 40 mph. What is the average speed for the entire round trip?',
    passage_id: null,
    answer_choices: { A: '48 mph', B: '50 mph', C: '52 mph', D: '45 mph' },
    correct_answer: 'A',
    distractor_analysis: {
      B: 'takes_simple_arithmetic_mean_60_plus_40_divided_by_2_ignoring_time_weighting',
      C: 'overestimates_by_using_incorrect_weighted_average',
      D: 'underestimates_by_taking_ratio_of_speeds_incorrectly'
    },
    is_ai_generated: false,
    tags: []
  }
];

const updated = [...q, ...newQuestions];
fs.writeFileSync\('./data/questions/seed-questions.json', JSON.stringify\(updated, null, 2\)\);
console.log\('Added', newQuestions.length, 'questions. Total:', updated.length\);
" 2>&1)

Bash(python3 - <<'EOF'
import struct

def make_pdf\(text\):
    """Create a minimal valid PDF containing the given text."""
    content = f"BT /F1 12 Tf 50 750 Td \({text}\) Tj ET"
    content_bytes = content.encode\(\)
    stream = f"""%PDF-1.4
1 0 obj<</Type /Catalog /Pages 2 0 R>>endobj
2 0 obj<</Type /Pages /Kids [3 0 R] /Count 1>>endobj
3 0 obj<</Type /Page /Parent 2 0 R /MediaBox [0 0 612 792] /Contents 4 0 R /Resources<</Font<</F1 5 0 R>>>>>>endobj
4 0 obj<</Length {len\(content_bytes\)}>>
stream
{content}
endstream
endobj
5 0 obj<</Type /Font /Subtype /Type1 /BaseFont /Helvetica>>endobj
xref
0 6
0000000000 65535 f 
trailer<</Size 6 /Root 1 0 R>>
startxref
9
%%EOF"""
    return stream.encode\(\)

# Questions PDF
q_text = "1. What is 2 + 2? \(A\) 3 \(B\) 4 \(C\) 5 \(D\) 6"
with open\('/tmp/test_questions.pdf', 'wb'\) as f:
    f.write\(make_pdf\(q_text\)\)

# Answers PDF
a_text = "1. B"
with open\('/tmp/test_answers.pdf', 'wb'\) as f:
    f.write\(make_pdf\(a_text\)\)

# Explanations PDF
e_text = "1. 2 + 2 = 4, so the answer is B."
with open\('/tmp/test_explanations.pdf', 'wb'\) as f:
    f.write\(make_pdf\(e_text\)\)

print\("Test PDFs created."\)
EOF)

# Answers PDF
pdf2 = FPDF\(\)
pdf2.add_page\(\)
pdf2.set_font\("Helvetica", size=12\)
pdf2.multi_cell\(0, 8, "Answer Key\\n\\n1. B\\n2. C"\)

pdf3.add_page\(\)
pdf3.set_font\("Helvetica", size=12\)

Bash(python3 - <<'EOF'
from fpdf import FPDF

class MyPDF\(FPDF\):
    pass

for name, content in [
    \("questions", "SAT Practice Test - Math Section\\n\\n1. What is the value of x if 2x + 4 = 14?\\n\(A\) 3\\n\(B\) 5\\n\(C\) 7\\n\(D\) 9\\n\\n2. A rectangle has length 8 and width 5. What is its area?\\n\(A\) 13\\n\(B\) 26\\n\(C\) 40\\n\(D\) 80"\),
    \("answers", "Answer Key\\n\\n1. B\\n2. C"\),
    \("explanations", "Explanations\\n\\n1. 2x + 4 = 14, so 2x = 10, x = 5. Correct answer: B.\\n2. Area = length x width = 8 x 5 = 40. Correct answer: C."\),
]:
    pdf = MyPDF\(\)
    pdf.add_page\(\)
    pdf.set_font\("Helvetica", size=12\)
    pdf.multi_cell\(0, 8, content\)
    # Use bytes output to check
    data = pdf.output\(\)
    with open\(f"/tmp/v2_{name}.pdf", "wb"\) as f:
        f.write\(bytes\(data\)\)
    print\(f"Created /tmp/v2_{name}.pdf, size={len\(data\)}"\)
EOF)

Bash(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/parent/upload-questions/parse \\
  -F "questions=@/Users/jasonfinkelstein/Downloads/sat-practice-test-4-digital.pdf;type=application/pdf" \\
  -F "answers=@/Users/jasonfinkelstein/Downloads/scoring-sat-practice-test-4-digital.pdf;type=application/pdf" \\
  -F "explanations=@/Users/jasonfinkelstein/Downloads/sat-practice-test-4-answers-digital.pdf;type=application/pdf" \\
  | python3 -c "
import sys, json
d = json.load\(sys.stdin\)

if 'error' in d:
    print\('ERROR:', d['error']\)
    sys.exit\(1\)

qs = d.get\('questions', []\)
warnings = d.get\('parse_warnings', []\)

print\(f'Questions parsed: {d[\\"total_parsed\\"]}'\)
print\(f'Warnings: {len\(warnings\)}'\)
if warnings:
    for w in warnings:
        print\(f'  - {w}'\)

print\(\)
print\('=== First 3 questions ==='\)
for q in qs[:3]:
    print\(f'Q{q[\\"question_number\\"]}: {q[\\"question_text\\"][:80]}...'\)
    print\(f'  Choices: {list\(q[\\"answer_choices\\"].keys\(\)\)}'\)
    print\(f'  Correct: {q[\\"correct_answer\\"]}'\)
    print\(f'  Section: {q[\\"section\\"]}'\)
    print\(f'  Explanation: {str\(q.get\(\\"explanation\\",\\"\\"\)\)[:80]}'\)
    print\(\)

print\('=== Last 3 questions ==='\)
for q in qs[-3:]:
    print\(f'Q{q[\\"question_number\\"]}: {q[\\"question_text\\"][:80]}...'\)
    print\(f'  Correct: {q[\\"correct_answer\\"]}  Section: {q[\\"section\\"]}'\)
    print\(\)

# Stats
sections = {}
for q in qs:
    s = q.get\('section','unknown'\)
    sections[s] = sections.get\(s,0\) + 1
print\('Section breakdown:', sections\)

answered = sum\(1 for q in qs if q.get\('correct_answer',''\).strip\(\)\)
explained = sum\(1 for q in qs if q.get\('explanation',''\).strip\(\)\)
print\(f'Questions with correct_answer: {answered}/{len\(qs\)}'\)
print\(f'Questions with explanation: {explained}/{len\(qs\)}'\)
" 2>&1)

Bash(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/parent/upload-questions/parse \\
  -F "questions=@/Users/jasonfinkelstein/Downloads/sat-practice-test-4-digital.pdf;type=application/pdf" \\
  -F "answers=@/Users/jasonfinkelstein/Downloads/scoring-sat-practice-test-4-digital.pdf;type=application/pdf" \\
  -F "explanations=@/Users/jasonfinkelstein/Downloads/sat-practice-test-4-answers-digital.pdf;type=application/pdf" \\
  | python3 -c "
import sys, json

d = json.load\(sys.stdin\)

if 'error' in d:
    print\('ERROR:', d['error']\)
    sys.exit\(1\)

qs = d.get\('questions', []\)
warnings = d.get\('parse_warnings', []\)

print\(f'=== RESULTS ==='\)
print\(f'Total questions parsed: {d[\\"total_parsed\\"]}'\)
print\(f'Warnings: {len\(warnings\)}'\)
for w in warnings:
    print\(f'  WARNING: {w}'\)

# Section breakdown
sections = {}
for q in qs:
    s = q.get\('section','?'\)
    sections[s] = sections.get\(s, 0\) + 1
print\(f'Section breakdown: {sections}'\)

# Answer / explanation coverage
answered  = sum\(1 for q in qs if str\(q.get\('correct_answer',''\)\).strip\(\)\)
explained = sum\(1 for q in qs if str\(q.get\('explanation',''\)\).strip\(\)\)
has_choices = sum\(1 for q in qs if q.get\('answer_choices'\)\)
print\(f'With correct_answer:  {answered}/{len\(qs\)}'\)
print\(f'With explanation:     {explained}/{len\(qs\)}'\)
print\(f'With answer_choices:  {has_choices}/{len\(qs\)}'\)

# Q number range
nums = [q.get\('question_number',0\) for q in qs]
print\(f'Question number range: {min\(nums\) if nums else \\"?\\"} – {max\(nums\) if nums else \\"?\\"}'\)

# Spot-check: first Q from each section
print\(\)
print\('=== SPOT CHECK ==='\)
for section in ['reading_writing', 'math']:
    sample = [q for q in qs if q.get\('section'\) == section]
    if sample:
        q = sample[0]
        print\(f'Q{q[\\"question_number\\"]} \({section}\): {str\(q[\\"question_text\\"]\)[:80]}...'\)
        print\(f'  Choices: {list\(\(q.get\(\\"answer_choices\\"\) or {}\).keys\(\)\)}  Correct: {q.get\(\\"correct_answer\\"\)}'\)
        print\(f'  Explanation: {str\(q.get\(\\"explanation\\",\\"\\"\)\)[:80]}'\)
        print\(\)
" 2>&1)

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<'EOF'
Build SAT Tutor Pro — full application from scratch

Complete Next.js app with AI-powered SAT tutoring features:

- Practice test and study session flows with adaptive question selection
- Spaced repetition, ELO scoring, and frustration detection
- Claude-powered tutor \(Socratic/direct modes\), error classification, pattern analysis
- PDF upload pipeline: server-side extraction \(unpdf\), module-chunked parsing, classify + confirm flow with duplicate detection
- Parent dashboard with question uploader and student progress view
- Progress, insights, review, and settings pages
- Supabase schema, seed data \(150 questions\), and all API routes
- shadcn/ui component library, Desmos embed, annotation toolbar

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")
